arts.ttl: Report: Eddie Howe eyed these two positions for January transfer window
arts.sum: Newcastle United manager Eddie Howe had his sights set on signing a right winger and a centre-back in the January transfer window, according to reports. Outlet Football Insider has claimed that the former Bournemouth man did want new additions. arts.ttl: Cheick Tiote's son included as Newcastle mascot for Carabao Cup semi-final on the 14th anniversary of his late father's famous goal in a 4-4 draw against Arsenal
arts.sum: Tiote memorably scored a stunning, 30-yard volley to complete one of the great Premier League comebacks, coming from 4-0 down at half-time against Arsenal to draw 4-4 back in 2011.

arts.ttl: “Just get on with business” – Collymore slams Arsenal for petty juvenile behaviour
arts.sum: Arsenal’s on-pitch behaviour against Man City may have got the thumbs up from the youngsters, but Stan Collymore has slammed the Gunners. Myles Lewis-Skelly was sent off against Wolves recently, but it was his antics when scoring in the game against the reigning champions that garnered as many column inches. arts.ttl: Lloyd Kelly Embraces New Challenge at Juventus
arts.sum: Lloyd Kelly, the latest addition to Juventus' defensive lineup, has expressed his enthusiasm about joining the Italian giants. The English defender, who completed his move from Newcastle United on deadline day, is eager to make his mark in Serie A. The post Lloyd Kelly Embraces New Challenge at Juventus appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Report: Four players Newcastle can sign for free after deadline day
arts.sum: Newcastle United had a quiet January transfer window, bringing in no signings, but that doesn’t necessarily mean their business is done just yet. Despite being linked with several players, the Magpies didn’t bring in any new signings before the window shut. arts.ttl: Is there VAR for Newcastle United vs Arsenal? Carabao Cup semi-final rules confirmed
arts.sum: Newcastle United will be bidding to reach the Carabao Cup final for the second time in three seasons this evening when they host Arsenal at St James' Park. Our award-winning The Everything is Black and White Podcast is the go-to Newcastle United show to bring you all the latest news, opinion, analysis and nostalgia about the club.

arts.ttl: Newcastle United unleash shock system change as big Kieran Trippier decision made
arts.sum: Howe also decided on picking a three-man central defensive unit of Fabian Schar, Sven Botman and Dan Burn with Lewis Hall left-back. UNITED: Dubravka, Trippier, Schar, Botman, Burn, Hall; Tonali, Bruno; Murphy, Isak, Gordon

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta confirms huge Arsenal decision is imminent as transfer plan explained
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has revealed that Arsenal's search for a new sporting director could be over "relatively quickly" - five months after Edu left the club. “We have the sporting director which is Jason, it’s clear and he’s been since the departure of Edu from day one he has been given full responsibility, all our support from that aspect and what the club wants to do after that is not on me,” said Arteta.

arts.ttl: Newcastle v Arsenal: Carabao Cup semi-final, second leg – live
arts.sum: Updates from St James’ Park (agg 2-0, 8pm KO) First leg report | Howe tells side to ‘leave a legacy’ “It is going to be another battle and we know how difficult it is to play there. But we have an amazing team and we trust ourselves. We have full confidence in our squad.” The tie will go to extra time if necessary, then penalties There’s no away goals rule VAR is being used Continue reading...
